examples.title: Basic Exception Handling
examples.explanation: Uses try block to wrap code, throw to raise std::invalid_argument, and catch to handle it. Multiple catch blocks allow handling different exception types.

examples.title: Multiple Catch Blocks with Rethrow
examples.description: Shows multiple catch handlers and exception rethrowing
examples.explanation: Demonstrates catch(int) in func, rethrow with throw;, and catch(...) in main to handle any uncaught exceptions.

examples.title: Custom Exception Class
examples.description: Creating user-defined exception inheriting from std::exception
examples.explanation: Custom exception class inherits from std::exception, overrides what() method. Can be caught by catching base class exception reference.

examples.title: Exception in Constructor
examples.description: Handling exceptions in constructor with function try block
examples.explanation: Demonstrates constructor throwing, function try block, and how member objects are properly cleaned up during stack unwinding.

examples.title: noexcept Function Specification
examples.description: Using noexcept to specify non-throwing functions
examples.explanation: noexcept guarantees function won't throw - if it does, std::terminate is called. noexcept(false) explicitly allows throwing.
